claim: SQLCipher AES-256 (or AES-256-GCM proxy when SQLCipher isn't installable) on Mem0's at-rest SQLite store adds at most 15% median-query latency overhead on a 1000-query retrieval workload vs unencrypted baseline. Pure latency measurement — accuracy regression is OUT of scope here (any deterministic encryption layer is round-trip-lossless).
source_for_claim: Spec v0.4 row 29: 'SQLCipher AES-256 with Argon2id-derived key from user passphrase (~5-15% latency overhead).' Bench supports SQLCipher canonical mode (in Docker) + AES-GCM-per-row proxy mode (local fallback) — proxy is a strict UPPER bound since per-row encryption is more expensive than SQLCipher's per-page approach.
comparison_anchor: plain SQLite (same schema, same indexes, same 1000-row corpus, same query workload)
decision_rule: If CONFIRMED in either mode, Zone A encryption ships as v1.x default. If REFUTED in proxy mode but encryption_mode tags 'aes-gcm-proxy', re-run via Docker with sqlcipher3 before declaring a real refutation — proxy is conservative. If REFUTED in canonical SQLCipher mode, encryption becomes opt-in with a documented warning and row 29 lock needs revising.
